title: polyscias willmottii
intro: Polyscias willmottii, also known as the Tī Kouka, is a native New Zealand tree with heart-shaped leaves and a broad, rounded crown. The medicinal properties of Polyscias willmottii have been used by the indigenous Maori people for centuries, with its bark, leaves, and roots being used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Polyscias willmottii include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of Polyscias willmottii include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or mixing them with other herbal remedies to create a topical treatment.
